notes: Manufacturer of Athlon XP processor line using GHz equivalency model numbering; later abandoned GHz equivalency and adopted Zen architecture to become major Intel competitor
Manufacturer of Pentium 4 processor line including Willamette and Northwood variants; AMD's primary competitor whose clock speeds were the reference for GHz equivalency ratings
Audited AMD's Athlon XP benchmark methodology using AICPA attestation standards; dissolved in 2002 following the Enron scandal; was one of the Big Five accounting firms
Industry consortium that developed SYSmark 2001 benchmark suite; SYSmark counted for over one-third of AMD's equivalency score; AMD failed to report Athlon XP 2000+ results to BAPCo
Developer of SPECcpu 2000 processor benchmark; Aberdeen recommended AMD examine SPECcpu 2000 for True Performance Initiative
Developer of 3DMark 2001 benchmark; renamed to Futuremark in 2002; acquired by UL (Underwriters Laboratories) in 2014
Publisher of Winstone 2001 and Winbench 2000 benchmarks used in AMD's equivalency methodology
Author of this white paper; technology market research firm that critically analyzed AMD's GHz equivalency methodology; later acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2008
Developer of Windows XP and Media Player used in AMD's benchmark suite; Media Player bug fix was selectively incorporated into AMD's results
Developer of Flash and Dreamweaver applications included in AMD's benchmark workload; acquired by Adobe Systems in 2005
Developer of Premiere and Photoshop applications included in AMD's content creation benchmark workload
Developer of Sound Forge audio editing application; Sound Forge product line acquired by Sony in 2003; company pivoted to webcasting/Mediasite
